    uid=4_117 StaticText "."
    uid=4_118 heading "How SKF Fits In" level="2"
    uid=4_119 link "Section titled “How SKF Fits In”" url="http://localhost:4321/architecture/#how-skf-fits-in"
      uid=4_120 StaticText "Section titled “How SKF Fits In”"
    uid=4_121 StaticText "SKF plugs into BMad the same way a specialist plugs into a team. It uses the same step-by-step workflow engine and shared standards, but focuses exclusively on skill compilation and quality assurance. That means you get "
    uid=4_122 StaticText "evidence-based agent skills"
    uid=4_123 StaticText ", "
    uid=4_124 StaticText "AST-verified instructions"
    uid=4_125 StaticText ", and "
    uid=4_126 StaticText "drift detection"
    uid=4_127 StaticText " that align with the rest of the BMad process."
    uid=4_128 heading "Architecture & Flow" level="2"
    uid=4_129 link "Section titled “Architecture & Flow”" url="http://localhost:4321/architecture/#architecture--flow"
      uid=4_130 StaticText "Section titled “Architecture & Flow”"
    uid=4_131 StaticText "BMad is a small "
    uid=4_132 StaticText "agent + workflow engine"
    uid=4_133 StaticText ". There is no external orchestrator — everything runs inside the LLM context window through structured instructions."
    uid=4_134 heading "Building Blocks" level="3"
    uid=4_135 link "Section titled “Building Blocks”" url="http://localhost:4321/architecture/#building-blocks"
      uid=4_136 StaticText "Section titled “Building Blocks”"
    uid=4_137 StaticText "Each workflow directory contains these files, and each has a specific job:"
    uid=4_138 StaticText "File"
    uid=4_139 StaticText "What it does"
    uid=4_140 StaticText "When it loads"
    uid=4_141 StaticText "forger.agent.yaml"
    uid=4_142 StaticText "Expert persona — identity, principles, critical actions, menu of triggers"
    uid=4_143 StaticText "First — always in context"
    uid=4_144 StaticText "workflow.md"
    uid=4_145 StaticText "Human-readable entry point — goals, mode menu (Create/Edit/Validate), routes to first step"
    uid=4_146 StaticText "Second — presents mode choice"
    uid=4_147 StaticText "steps-c/*.md"
    uid=4_148 StaticText "Create"
    uid=4_149 StaticText " steps — primary execution, 4-9 sequential files"
    uid=4_150 StaticText "One at a time (just-in-time)"
    uid=4_151 StaticText "data/*.md"
    uid=4_152 StaticText "Workflow-specific reference data — schemas, heuristics, rules, patterns"
    uid=4_153 StaticText "Read by steps on demand"
    uid=4_154 StaticText "templates/*.md"
    uid=4_155 StaticText "Output skeletons with placeholder vars — steps fill these in to produce the final artifact"
    uid=4_156 StaticText "Read by steps when generating output"
    uid=4_157 StaticText "skf-knowledge-index.csv"
    uid=4_158 StaticText "Knowledge fragment index — id, name, tags, tier, file path"
    uid=4_159 StaticText "Read by steps to decide which fragments to load"
    uid=4_160 StaticText "knowledge/*.md"
    uid=4_161 StaticText "10 reusable fragments — cross-cutting principles and patterns (e.g., "
    uid=4_162 StaticText "zero-hallucination.md"
    uid=4_163 StaticText ", "
    uid=4_164 StaticText "confidence-tiers.md"
    uid=4_165 StaticText ")"
    uid=4_166 StaticText "Selectively read into context when a step directs"
    uid=4_167 graphics-document roledescription="flowchart-v2"
      uid=4_168 StaticText "User"
      uid=4_169 StaticText "Agent Persona"
      uid=4_170 StaticText "Workflow Entry: workflow.md"
      uid=4_171 StaticText "Step Files: steps-c/"
      uid=4_172 StaticText "Knowledge Fragments"
      uid=4_173 LineBreak "
"
      uid=4_174 StaticText "skf-knowledge-index.csv → knowledge/*.md"
      uid=4_175 StaticText "Data & Templates"
      uid=4_176 LineBreak "
"
      uid=4_177 StaticText "data/*.md, templates/*.md"
      uid=4_178 StaticText "Outputs: skills/reports"
      uid=4_179 LineBreak "
"
      uid=4_180 StaticText "when a step writes output"
    uid=4_181 heading "How It Works at Runtime" level="3"
    uid=4_182 link "Section titled “How It Works at Runtime”" url="http://localhost:4321/architecture/#how-it-works-at-runtime"
      uid=4_183 StaticText "Section titled “How It Works at Runtime”"
    uid=4_184 StaticText "Trigger"
    uid=4_185 StaticText " — User types "
    uid=4_186 StaticText "@Ferris CS"
    uid=4_187 StaticText " (or fuzzy match like "
    uid=4_188 StaticText "create-skill"
    uid=4_189 StaticText "). The agent menu in "
    uid=4_190 StaticText "forger.agent.yaml"
    uid=4_191 StaticText " maps the trigger to the workflow path."
    uid=4_192 StaticText "Agent loads"
    uid=4_193 StaticText " — "
    uid=4_194 StaticText "forger.agent.yaml"
    uid=4_195 StaticText " injects the persona (identity, principles, critical actions) into the context window. Sidecar files ("
    uid=4_196 StaticText "forge-tier.yaml"
    uid=4_197 StaticText ", "
    uid=4_198 StaticText "preferences.yaml"
    uid=4_199 StaticText ") are loaded for persistent state."
    uid=4_200 StaticText "Workflow loads"
    uid=4_201 StaticText " — "
    uid=4_202 StaticText "workflow.md"
    uid=4_203 StaticText " presents the mode choice and routes to the first step file."
    uid=4_204 StaticText "Step-by-step execution"
    uid=4_205 StaticText " — Only the current step file is in context (just-in-time loading). Each step explicitly names the next one. The LLM reads, executes, saves output, then loads the next step. No future steps are ever preloaded."
    uid=4_206 StaticText "Knowledge injection"
    uid=4_207 StaticText " — Steps consult "
    uid=4_208 StaticText "skf-knowledge-index.csv"
    uid=4_209 StaticText " and selectively load fragments from "
    uid=4_210 StaticText "knowledge/"
    uid=4_211 StaticText " by tags and relevance. Cross-cutting principles (zero hallucination, confidence tiers, provenance) are loaded only when a step directs — not preloaded."
    uid=4_212 StaticText "Data injection"
    uid=4_213 StaticText " — Steps read "
    uid=4_214 StaticText "data/*.md"
    uid=4_215 StaticText " files as needed (schemas, heuristics, extraction patterns). This is deliberate context engineering: only the data relevant to the current step enters the context window."
    uid=4_216 StaticText "Templates"
    uid=4_217 StaticText " — When a step produces output (e.g., a skill brief or test report), it reads the template file and fills in placeholders with computed results. The template provides consistent structure; the step provides the content."
    uid=4_218 StaticText "Progress tracking"
    uid=4_219 StaticText " — Each step appends to an output file with state tracking. Resume mode reads this state and routes to the next incomplete step."
    uid=4_220 heading "Ferris Operating Modes" level="3"
    uid=4_221 link "Section titled “Ferris Operating Modes”" url="http://localhost:4321/architecture/#ferris-operating-modes"
      uid=4_222 StaticText "Section titled “Ferris Operating Modes”"
    uid=4_223 StaticText "Ferris operates in four workflow-driven modes (mode is determined by which workflow is running, not conversation state):"
    uid=4_224 StaticText "Mode"
    uid=4_225 StaticText "Workflows"
    uid=4_226 StaticText "Behavior"
    uid=4_227 StaticText "Architect"
    uid=4_228 StaticText "SF, AN, BS, CS, QS, SS"
    uid=4_229 StaticText "Exploratory, assembling — discovers structure and scope"
    uid=4_230 StaticText "Surgeon"
    uid=4_231 StaticText "US"
    uid=4_232 StaticText "Precise, preserving — extracts and compiles with provenance"
    uid=4_233 StaticText "Audit"
    uid=4_234 StaticText "AS, TS"
    uid=4_235 StaticText "Judgmental, scoring — evaluates quality and detects drift"
    uid=4_236 StaticText "Delivery"
    uid=4_237 StaticText "EX"
    uid=4_238 StaticText "Packaging, ecosystem-ready — bundles for distribution"
    uid=4_239 heading "Why Another Tool?" level="2"
    uid=4_240 link "Section titled “Why Another Tool?”" url="http://localhost:4321/architecture/#why-another-tool"
      uid=4_241 StaticText "Section titled “Why Another Tool?”"
    uid=4_242 StaticText "AI agents hallucinate APIs. Not sometimes — constantly. The table below shows why every existing approach fails at scale:"
    uid=4_243 StaticText "Approach"
    uid=4_244 StaticText "Strength"
    uid=4_245 StaticText "Fatal Flaw"
    uid=4_246 StaticText "npx skills init"
    uid=4_247 StaticText "Format compliant"
    uid=4_248 StaticText "Empty shell. 0% intelligence."
    uid=4_249 StaticText "LLM Summarization"
    uid=4_250 StaticText "High semantic context"
    uid=4_251 StaticText "Hallucination. Guesses parameters. No grounding."
    uid=4_252 StaticText "RAG / Context stuffing"
    uid=4_253 StaticText "Good retrieval"
    uid=4_254 StaticText "Fragmented. Finds snippets, fails to synthesize."
    uid=4_255 StaticText "Manual Authoring"
    uid=4_256 StaticText "High initial quality"
    uid=4_257 StaticText "Drift. Doesn’t scale."
    uid=4_258 StaticText "Copilot/Cursor built-in"
    uid=4_259 StaticText "Convenient"
    uid=4_260 StaticText "Generic. Doesn’t know YOUR integration patterns."
    uid=4_261 StaticText "Skill Forge"
    uid=4_262 StaticText "Structural truth + automation"
    uid=4_263 StaticText "Rigid. (Feature, not bug.)"
    uid=4_264 StaticText "SKF solves this by mechanically extracting function signatures, type definitions, and usage patterns from code repositories — and enriching them with documentation and developer discourse — then compiling everything into verifiable, version-pinned skills that comply with the "
    uid=4_265 link "agentskills.io specification" url="https://agentskills.io/specification"
      uid=4_266 StaticText "agentskills.io specification"
    uid=4_267 StaticText "."
    uid=4_268 heading "Progressive Capability Model" level="2"
    uid=4_269 link "Section titled “Progressive Capability Model”" url="http://localhost:4321/architecture/#progressive-capability-model"
      uid=4_270 StaticText "Section titled “Progressive Capability Model”"
    uid=4_271 StaticText "SKF uses an additive tier model. Each tier is the previous tier plus one tool. You never lose capability by adding a tool."
    uid=4_272 StaticText "Tier"
    uid=4_273 StaticText "Tools"
    uid=4_274 StaticText "What You Get"
    uid=4_275 StaticText "Quick"
    uid=4_276 StaticText "gh_bridge"
    uid=4_277 StaticText " + "
    uid=4_278 StaticText "skill-check"
    uid=4_279 StaticText " + "
    uid=4_280 StaticText "tessl"
    uid=4_281 StaticText "Source reading + spec validation + content quality review. Best-effort skills in under a minute."
    uid=4_282 StaticText "Forge"
    uid=4_283 StaticText "+ "
    uid=4_284 StaticText "ast_bridge"
    uid=4_285 StaticText "Structural truth. AST-verified signatures. Co-import detection. T1 confidence."
    uid=4_286 StaticText "Deep"
    uid=4_287 StaticText "+ "
    uid=4_288 StaticText "qmd_bridge"
    uid=4_289 StaticText "Knowledge search. Temporal provenance. Drift detection. Full intelligence."
    uid=4_290 StaticText "Setup detects your installed tools and sets your tier automatically:"
    uid=4_291 StaticText "@Ferris SF"
    uid=4_292 generic live="polite" relevant="additions text"
    uid=4_293 button "Copy to clipboard"
    uid=4_294 StaticText "Forge initialized. Tools: gh, ast-grep, QMD. Tier: Deep. Ready."
    uid=4_295 generic live="polite" relevant="additions text"
    uid=4_296 button "Copy to clipboard"
    uid=4_297 StaticText "Don’t have ast-grep or QMD yet? No problem — Quick mode works with just the GitHub CLI. Install tools later; your tier upgrades automatically."
    uid=4_298 heading "Tier Override — Comparing Output Across Tiers" level="3"
    uid=4_299 link "Section titled “Tier Override — Comparing Output Across Tiers”" url="http://localhost:4321/architecture/#tier-override--comparing-output-across-tiers"
      uid=4_300 StaticText "Section titled “Tier Override — Comparing Output Across Tiers”"
    uid=4_301 StaticText "You can force a specific tier by setting "
    uid=4_302 StaticText "tier_override"
    uid=4_303 StaticText " in your preferences file ("
    uid=4_304 StaticText "_bmad/_memory/forger-sidecar/preferences.yaml"
    uid=4_305 StaticText "):"
    uid=4_306 StaticText "# Force Forge tier regardless of detected tools"
    uid=4_307 StaticText "tier_override"
    uid=4_308 StaticText ": "
    uid=4_309 StaticText "Forge"
    uid=4_310 generic live="polite" relevant="additions text"
    uid=4_311 button "Copy to clipboard"
    uid=4_312 StaticText "This is useful for comparing skill quality across tiers for the same target:"
    uid=4_313 StaticText "# 1. Set tier_override: Quick in preferences.yaml"
    uid=4_314 StaticText "@Ferris CS                # compile at Quick tier"
    uid=4_315 StaticText "
"
    uid=4_316 StaticText "# 2. Change to tier_override: Forge"
    uid=4_317 StaticText "@Ferris CS                # recompile at Forge tier — compare output"
    uid=4_318 StaticText "
"
    uid=4_319 StaticText "# 3. Reset to tier_override: ~ (auto-detect)"
    uid=4_320 generic live="polite" relevant="additions text"
    uid=4_321 button "Copy to clipboard"
    uid=4_322 StaticText "Set "
    uid=4_323 StaticText "tier_override"
    uid=4_324 StaticText " to "
    uid=4_325 StaticText "Quick"
    uid=4_326 StaticText ", "
    uid=4_327 StaticText "Forge"
    uid=4_328 StaticText ", or "
    uid=4_329 StaticText "Deep"
    uid=4_330 StaticText ". Set to "
    uid=4_331 StaticText "~"
    uid=4_332 StaticText " (null) to return to auto-detection. The override is respected by all tier-aware workflows (CS, SS, US, AS, TS)."
    uid=4_333 heading "Confidence Tiers" level="2"
    uid=4_334 link "Section titled “Confidence Tiers”" url="http://localhost:4321/architecture/#confidence-tiers"
      uid=4_335 StaticText "Section titled “Confidence Tiers”"
    uid=4_336 StaticText "Every claim in a generated skill carries a confidence tier that traces to its source:"
    uid=4_337 StaticText "Tier"
    uid=4_338 StaticText "Source"
    uid=4_339 StaticText "Tool"
    uid=4_340 StaticText "What It Means"
    uid=4_341 StaticText "T1"
    uid=4_342 StaticText "AST extraction"
    uid=4_343 StaticText "ast_bridge"
    uid=4_344 StaticText "Current code, structurally verified. Immutable for that version."
    uid=4_345 StaticText "T2"
    uid=4_346 StaticText "QMD evidence / source reading"
    uid=4_347 StaticText "qmd_bridge"
    uid=4_348 StaticText " / "
    uid=4_349 StaticText "gh_bridge"
    uid=4_350 StaticText "Historical + planned context (issues, PRs, changelogs, docs)."
    uid=4_351 StaticText "T3"
    uid=4_352 StaticText "External documentation"
    uid=4_353 StaticText "doc_fetcher"
    uid=4_354 StaticText "External, untrusted. Quarantined."
    uid=4_355 heading "Temporal Provenance" level="3"
    uid=4_356 link "Section titled “Temporal Provenance”" url="http://localhost:4321/architecture/#temporal-provenance"
      uid=4_357 StaticText "Section titled “Temporal Provenance”"
    uid=4_358 StaticText "Confidence tiers map to temporal scopes:"
    uid=4_359 StaticText "T1-now (instructions):"
    uid=4_360 StaticText " What ast-grep sees in the checked-out code. This is what your agent executes."
    uid=4_361 StaticText "T2-past (annotations):"
    uid=4_362 StaticText " Closed issues, merged PRs, changelogs — why the API looks the way it does."
    uid=4_363 StaticText "T2-future (annotations):"
    uid=4_364 StaticText " Open PRs, deprecation warnings, RFCs — what’s coming."
    uid=4_365 StaticText "Progressive disclosure controls how much context surfaces at each level:"
    uid=4_366 StaticText "Output"
    uid=4_367 StaticText "Content"
    uid=4_368 StaticText "context-snippet.md"
    uid=4_369 StaticText "T1-now only — compressed, always-on"
    uid=4_370 StaticText "SKILL.md"
    uid=4_371 StaticText "T1-now + lightweight T2 annotations"
    uid=4_372 StaticText "references/"
    uid=4_373 StaticText "Full temporal context with all tiers"
    uid=4_374 heading "Tier Constrains Authority" level="3"
    uid=4_375 link "Section titled “Tier Constrains Authority”" url="http://localhost:4321/architecture/#tier-constrains-authority"
      uid=4_376 StaticText "Section titled “Tier Constrains Authority”"

    uid=4_398 heading "Output Architecture" level="2"
    uid=4_399 link "Section titled “Output Architecture”" url="http://localhost:4321/architecture/#output-architecture"
      uid=4_400 StaticText "Section titled “Output Architecture”"
    uid=4_401 heading "Per-Skill Output" level="3"

      uid=4_403 StaticText "Section titled “Per-Skill Output”"
    uid=4_404 StaticText "Every generated skill produces a self-contained directory:"

    uid=4_406 StaticText "├── SKILL.md              # Active skill (loaded on trigger)"
    uid=4_407 StaticText "├── context-snippet.md    # Passive context (compressed, always-on)"

    uid=4_410 StaticText "    "

    uid=4_412 StaticText "    "

    uid=4_556 heading "Stack Skill Output" level="3"

      uid=4_558 StaticText "Section titled “Stack Skill Output”"
    uid=4_559 StaticText "Stack skills map how your dependencies interact — shared types, co-import patterns, integration points:"

    uid=4_741 heading "Key Design Decisions" level="2"

      uid=4_743 StaticText "Section titled “Key Design Decisions”"
    uid=4_744 StaticText "Decision"
    uid=4_745 StaticText "Rationale"
    uid=4_746 StaticText "Solo agent (Ferris), not multi-agent"
    uid=4_747 StaticText "One domain (skill compilation) doesn’t benefit from handoffs. Shared knowledge base (AST patterns, provenance maps) is the core asset."
    uid=4_748 StaticText "Workflows drive modes, not conversation"
    uid=4_749 StaticText "Ferris doesn’t auto-switch based on question content. Invoke a workflow to change mode. Predictable behavior."
    uid=4_750 StaticText "Hub-and-spoke cross-knowledge"
    uid=4_751 StaticText "Each skill has one primary source. Cross-repo references use inline summary + pointer + "
    uid=4_752 StaticText "[XREF:repo:file:line]"
    uid=4_753 StaticText " provenance tag."
    uid=4_754 StaticText "Stack skill = compositional"
    uid=4_755 StaticText "SKILL.md is the integration layer. references/ contains per-library + integration pairs. Partial regeneration on dependency updates."
    uid=4_756 StaticText "Snippet updates only at export"
    uid=4_757 StaticText "Create/update are draft operations. Export publishes to skills/ and CLAUDE.md. No half-baked snippets."
    uid=4_758 StaticText "Bundle spec with opt-in update"
    uid=4_759 StaticText "Offline-capable. 90-day staleness warning. "
    uid=4_760 StaticText "setup-forge --update-spec"
    uid=4_761 StaticText " fetches latest."

    uid=5_100 heading "How It Works" level="1"
    uid=5_101 heading "How It Works" level="1"
    uid=5_102 link "Section titled “How It Works”" url="http://localhost:4321/architecture/#how-it-works"
      uid=5_103 StaticText "Section titled “How It Works”"
    uid=5_104 StaticText "This page is for people who want to understand how SKF works under the hood. It covers the BMad framework, workflow architecture, capability tiers, output format, tool ecosystem, and key design decisions. For plain-English definitions of key terms, see "
    uid=5_105 link "Concepts" url="http://localhost:4321/concepts/"
      uid=5_106 StaticText "Concepts"
    uid=5_107 StaticText "."
    uid=5_108 heading "How BMad Works" level="2"
    uid=5_109 link "Section titled “How BMad Works”" url="http://localhost:4321/architecture/#how-bmad-works"
      uid=5_110 StaticText "Section titled “How BMad Works”"
    uid=5_111 StaticText "BMad works because it turns big, fuzzy work into "
    uid=5_112 StaticText "repeatable workflows"
    uid=5_113 StaticText ". Each workflow is broken into small steps with clear instructions, so the AI follows the same path every time. It also uses a "
    uid=5_114 StaticText "shared knowledge base"
    uid=5_115 StaticText " (standards and patterns) so outputs are consistent, not random. In short: "
    uid=5_116 StaticText "structured steps + shared standards = reliable results"
    uid=5_117 StaticText "."
    uid=5_118 heading "How SKF Fits In" level="2"
    uid=5_119 link "Section titled “How SKF Fits In”" url="http://localhost:4321/architecture/#how-skf-fits-in"
      uid=5_120 StaticText "Section titled “How SKF Fits In”"
    uid=5_121 StaticText "SKF plugs into BMad the same way a specialist plugs into a team. It uses the same step-by-step workflow engine and shared standards, but focuses exclusively on skill compilation and quality assurance. That means you get "
    uid=5_122 StaticText "evidence-based agent skills"
    uid=5_123 StaticText ", "
    uid=5_124 StaticText "AST-verified instructions"
    uid=5_125 StaticText ", and "
    uid=5_126 StaticText "drift detection"
    uid=5_127 StaticText " that align with the rest of the BMad process."
    uid=5_128 heading "Architecture & Flow" level="2"
    uid=5_129 link "Section titled “Architecture & Flow”" url="http://localhost:4321/architecture/#architecture--flow"
      uid=5_130 StaticText "Section titled “Architecture & Flow”"
    uid=5_131 StaticText "BMad is a small "
    uid=5_132 StaticText "agent + workflow engine"
    uid=5_133 StaticText ". There is no external orchestrator — everything runs inside the LLM context window through structured instructions."
    uid=5_134 heading "Building Blocks" level="3"
    uid=5_135 link "Section titled “Building Blocks”" url="http://localhost:4321/architecture/#building-blocks"
      uid=5_136 StaticText "Section titled “Building Blocks”"
    uid=5_137 StaticText "Each workflow directory contains these files, and each has a specific job:"
    uid=5_138 StaticText "File"
    uid=5_139 StaticText "What it does"
    uid=5_140 StaticText "When it loads"
    uid=5_141 StaticText "forger.agent.yaml"
    uid=5_142 StaticText "Expert persona — identity, principles, critical actions, menu of triggers"
    uid=5_143 StaticText "First — always in context"
    uid=5_144 StaticText "workflow.md"
    uid=5_145 StaticText "Human-readable entry point — goals, mode menu (Create/Edit/Validate), routes to first step"
    uid=5_146 StaticText "Second — presents mode choice"
    uid=5_147 StaticText "steps-c/*.md"
    uid=5_148 StaticText "Create"
    uid=5_149 StaticText " steps — primary execution, 4-9 sequential files"
    uid=5_150 StaticText "One at a time (just-in-time)"
    uid=5_151 StaticText "data/*.md"
    uid=5_152 StaticText "Workflow-specific reference data — schemas, heuristics, rules, patterns"
    uid=5_153 StaticText "Read by steps on demand"
    uid=5_154 StaticText "templates/*.md"
    uid=5_155 StaticText "Output skeletons with placeholder vars — steps fill these in to produce the final artifact"
    uid=5_156 StaticText "Read by steps when generating output"
    uid=5_157 StaticText "skf-knowledge-index.csv"
    uid=5_158 StaticText "Knowledge fragment index — id, name, tags, tier, file path"
    uid=5_159 StaticText "Read by steps to decide which fragments to load"
    uid=5_160 StaticText "knowledge/*.md"
    uid=5_161 StaticText "10 reusable fragments — cross-cutting principles and patterns (e.g., "
    uid=5_162 StaticText "zero-hallucination.md"
    uid=5_163 StaticText ", "
    uid=5_164 StaticText "confidence-tiers.md"
    uid=5_165 StaticText ")"
    uid=5_166 StaticText "Selectively read into context when a step directs"
    uid=5_167 graphics-document roledescription="flowchart-v2"
      uid=5_168 StaticText "User"
      uid=5_169 StaticText "Agent Persona"
      uid=5_170 StaticText "Workflow Entry: workflow.md"
      uid=5_171 StaticText "Step Files: steps-c/"
      uid=5_172 StaticText "Knowledge Fragments"
      uid=5_173 LineBreak "
"
      uid=5_174 StaticText "skf-knowledge-index.csv → knowledge/*.md"
      uid=5_175 StaticText "Data & Templates"
      uid=5_176 LineBreak "
"
      uid=5_177 StaticText "data/*.md, templates/*.md"
      uid=5_178 StaticText "Outputs: skills/reports"
      uid=5_179 LineBreak "
"
      uid=5_180 StaticText "when a step writes output"
    uid=5_181 heading "How It Works at Runtime" level="3"
    uid=5_182 link "Section titled “How It Works at Runtime”" url="http://localhost:4321/architecture/#how-it-works-at-runtime"
      uid=5_183 StaticText "Section titled “How It Works at Runtime”"
    uid=5_184 StaticText "Trigger"
    uid=5_185 StaticText " — User types "
    uid=5_186 StaticText "@Ferris CS"
    uid=5_187 StaticText " (or fuzzy match like "
    uid=5_188 StaticText "create-skill"
    uid=5_189 StaticText "). The agent menu in "
    uid=5_190 StaticText "forger.agent.yaml"
    uid=5_191 StaticText " maps the trigger to the workflow path."
    uid=5_192 StaticText "Agent loads"
    uid=5_193 StaticText " — "
    uid=5_194 StaticText "forger.agent.yaml"
    uid=5_195 StaticText " injects the persona (identity, principles, critical actions) into the context window. Sidecar files ("
    uid=5_196 StaticText "forge-tier.yaml"
    uid=5_197 StaticText ", "
    uid=5_198 StaticText "preferences.yaml"
    uid=5_199 StaticText ") are loaded for persistent state."
    uid=5_200 StaticText "Workflow loads"
    uid=5_201 StaticText " — "
    uid=5_202 StaticText "workflow.md"
    uid=5_203 StaticText " presents the mode choice and routes to the first step file."
    uid=5_204 StaticText "Step-by-step execution"
    uid=5_205 StaticText " — Only the current step file is in context (just-in-time loading). Each step explicitly names the next one. The LLM reads, executes, saves output, then loads the next step. No future steps are ever preloaded."
    uid=5_206 StaticText "Knowledge injection"
    uid=5_207 StaticText " — Steps consult "
    uid=5_208 StaticText "skf-knowledge-index.csv"
    uid=5_209 StaticText " and selectively load fragments from "
    uid=5_210 StaticText "knowledge/"
    uid=5_211 StaticText " by tags and relevance. Cross-cutting principles (zero hallucination, confidence tiers, provenance) are loaded only when a step directs — not preloaded."
    uid=5_212 StaticText "Data injection"
    uid=5_213 StaticText " — Steps read "
    uid=5_214 StaticText "data/*.md"
    uid=5_215 StaticText " files as needed (schemas, heuristics, extraction patterns). This is deliberate context engineering: only the data relevant to the current step enters the context window."
    uid=5_216 StaticText "Templates"
    uid=5_217 StaticText " — When a step produces output (e.g., a skill brief or test report), it reads the template file and fills in placeholders with computed results. The template provides consistent structure; the step provides the content."
    uid=5_218 StaticText "Progress tracking"
    uid=5_219 StaticText " — Each step appends to an output file with state tracking. Resume mode reads this state and routes to the next incomplete step."
    uid=5_220 heading "Ferris Operating Modes" level="3"
    uid=5_221 link "Section titled “Ferris Operating Modes”" url="http://localhost:4321/architecture/#ferris-operating-modes"
      uid=5_222 StaticText "Section titled “Ferris Operating Modes”"
    uid=5_223 StaticText "Ferris operates in four workflow-driven modes (mode is determined by which workflow is running, not conversation state):"
    uid=5_224 StaticText "Mode"
    uid=5_225 StaticText "Workflows"
    uid=5_226 StaticText "Behavior"
    uid=5_227 StaticText "Architect"
    uid=5_228 StaticText "SF, AN, BS, CS, QS, SS"
    uid=5_229 StaticText "Exploratory, assembling — discovers structure and scope"
    uid=5_230 StaticText "Surgeon"
    uid=5_231 StaticText "US"
    uid=5_232 StaticText "Precise, preserving — extracts and compiles with provenance"
    uid=5_233 StaticText "Audit"
    uid=5_234 StaticText "AS, TS"
    uid=5_235 StaticText "Judgmental, scoring — evaluates quality and detects drift"
    uid=5_236 StaticText "Delivery"
    uid=5_237 StaticText "EX"
    uid=5_238 StaticText "Packaging, ecosystem-ready — bundles for distribution"
    uid=5_239 heading "Why Another Tool?" level="2"
    uid=5_240 link "Section titled “Why Another Tool?”" url="http://localhost:4321/architecture/#why-another-tool"
      uid=5_241 StaticText "Section titled “Why Another Tool?”"
    uid=5_242 StaticText "AI agents hallucinate APIs. Not sometimes — constantly. The table below shows why every existing approach fails at scale:"
    uid=5_243 StaticText "Approach"
    uid=5_244 StaticText "Strength"
    uid=5_245 StaticText "Fatal Flaw"
    uid=5_246 StaticText "npx skills init"
    uid=5_247 StaticText "Format compliant"
    uid=5_248 StaticText "Empty shell. 0% intelligence."
    uid=5_249 StaticText "LLM Summarization"
    uid=5_250 StaticText "High semantic context"
    uid=5_251 StaticText "Hallucination. Guesses parameters. No grounding."
    uid=5_252 StaticText "RAG / Context stuffing"
    uid=5_253 StaticText "Good retrieval"
    uid=5_254 StaticText "Fragmented. Finds snippets, fails to synthesize."
    uid=5_255 StaticText "Manual Authoring"
    uid=5_256 StaticText "High initial quality"
    uid=5_257 StaticText "Drift. Doesn’t scale."
    uid=5_258 StaticText "Copilot/Cursor built-in"
    uid=5_259 StaticText "Convenient"
    uid=5_260 StaticText "Generic. Doesn’t know YOUR integration patterns."
    uid=5_261 StaticText "Skill Forge"
    uid=5_262 StaticText "Structural truth + automation"
    uid=5_263 StaticText "Rigid. (Feature, not bug.)"
    uid=5_264 StaticText "SKF solves this by mechanically extracting function signatures, type definitions, and usage patterns from code repositories — and enriching them with documentation and developer discourse — then compiling everything into verifiable, version-pinned skills that comply with the "
    uid=5_265 link "agentskills.io specification" url="https://agentskills.io/specification"
      uid=5_266 StaticText "agentskills.io specification"
    uid=5_267 StaticText "."
    uid=5_268 heading "Progressive Capability Model" level="2"
    uid=5_269 link "Section titled “Progressive Capability Model”" url="http://localhost:4321/architecture/#progressive-capability-model"
      uid=5_270 StaticText "Section titled “Progressive Capability Model”"
    uid=5_271 StaticText "SKF uses an additive tier model. Each tier is the previous tier plus one tool. You never lose capability by adding a tool."
    uid=5_272 StaticText "Tier"
    uid=5_273 StaticText "Tools"
    uid=5_274 StaticText "What You Get"
    uid=5_275 StaticText "Quick"
    uid=5_276 StaticText "gh_bridge"
    uid=5_277 StaticText " + "
    uid=5_278 StaticText "skill-check"
    uid=5_279 StaticText " + "
    uid=5_280 StaticText "tessl"
    uid=5_281 StaticText "Source reading + spec validation + content quality review. Best-effort skills in under a minute."
    uid=5_282 StaticText "Forge"
    uid=5_283 StaticText "+ "
    uid=5_284 StaticText "ast_bridge"
    uid=5_285 StaticText "Structural truth. AST-verified signatures. Co-import detection. T1 confidence."
    uid=5_286 StaticText "Deep"
    uid=5_287 StaticText "+ "
    uid=5_288 StaticText "qmd_bridge"
    uid=5_289 StaticText "Knowledge search. Temporal provenance. Drift detection. Full intelligence."
    uid=5_290 StaticText "Setup detects your installed tools and sets your tier automatically:"
    uid=5_291 StaticText "@Ferris SF"
    uid=5_292 generic live="polite" relevant="additions text"
    uid=5_293 button "Copy to clipboard"
    uid=5_294 StaticText "Forge initialized. Tools: gh, ast-grep, QMD. Tier: Deep. Ready."
    uid=5_295 generic live="polite" relevant="additions text"
    uid=5_296 button "Copy to clipboard"
    uid=5_297 StaticText "Don’t have ast-grep or QMD yet? No problem — Quick mode works with just the GitHub CLI. Install tools later; your tier upgrades automatically."
    uid=5_298 heading "Tier Override — Comparing Output Across Tiers" level="3"
    uid=5_299 link "Section titled “Tier Override — Comparing Output Across Tiers”" url="http://localhost:4321/architecture/#tier-override--comparing-output-across-tiers"
      uid=5_300 StaticText "Section titled “Tier Override — Comparing Output Across Tiers”"
    uid=5_301 StaticText "You can force a specific tier by setting "
    uid=5_302 StaticText "tier_override"
    uid=5_303 StaticText " in your preferences file ("
    uid=5_304 StaticText "_bmad/_memory/forger-sidecar/preferences.yaml"
    uid=5_305 StaticText "):"
    uid=5_306 StaticText "# Force Forge tier regardless of detected tools"
    uid=5_307 StaticText "tier_override"

    uid=5_309 StaticText "Forge"
    uid=5_310 generic live="polite" relevant="additions text"
    uid=5_311 button "Copy to clipboard"
    uid=5_312 StaticText "This is useful for comparing skill quality across tiers for the same target:"

    uid=5_315 StaticText "
"

    uid=5_318 StaticText "
"
    uid=5_319 StaticText "# 3. Reset to tier_override: ~ (auto-detect)"
    uid=5_320 generic live="polite" relevant="additions text"

    uid=5_333 heading "Confidence Tiers" level="2"
    uid=5_334 link "Section titled “Confidence Tiers”" url="http://localhost:4321/architecture/#confidence-tiers"
      uid=5_335 StaticText "Section titled “Confidence Tiers”"

    uid=5_339 StaticText "Tool"
    uid=5_340 StaticText "What It Means"
    uid=5_341 StaticText "T1"

    uid=5_355 heading "Temporal Provenance" level="3"
    uid=5_356 link "Section titled “Temporal Provenance”" url="http://localhost:4321/architecture/#temporal-provenance"
      uid=5_357 StaticText "Section titled “Temporal Provenance”"
    uid=5_358 StaticText "Confidence tiers map to temporal scopes:"
    uid=5_359 StaticText "T1-now (instructions):"
    uid=5_360 StaticText " What ast-grep sees in the checked-out code. This is what your agent executes."
    uid=5_361 StaticText "T2-past (annotations):"
    uid=5_362 StaticText " Closed issues, merged PRs, changelogs — why the API looks the way it does."
    uid=5_363 StaticText "T2-future (annotations):"
    uid=5_364 StaticText " Open PRs, deprecation warnings, RFCs — what’s coming."
    uid=5_365 StaticText "Progressive disclosure controls how much context surfaces at each level:"
    uid=5_366 StaticText "Output"
    uid=5_367 StaticText "Content"
    uid=5_368 StaticText "context-snippet.md"
    uid=5_369 StaticText "T1-now only — compressed, always-on"
    uid=5_370 StaticText "SKILL.md"
    uid=5_371 StaticText "T1-now + lightweight T2 annotations"
    uid=5_372 StaticText "references/"
    uid=5_373 StaticText "Full temporal context with all tiers"

    uid=5_404 StaticText "Every generated skill produces a self-contained directory:"

    uid=5_407 StaticText "├── context-snippet.md    # Passive context (compressed, always-on)"

    uid=5_409 StaticText "└── references/           # Progressive disclosure"
    uid=5_410 StaticText "    "
    uid=5_411 StaticText "├── {function-a}.md"
    uid=5_412 StaticText "    "
    uid=5_413 StaticText "├── {function-b}.md"
    uid=5_414 StaticText "    "
    uid=5_415 StaticText "└── integrations/     # Stack skills only"

    uid=5_417 StaticText "├── auth-db.md"
    uid=5_418 StaticText "        "
    uid=5_419 StaticText "└── pwa-auth.md"

    uid=5_741 heading "Key Design Decisions" level="2"

      uid=5_743 StaticText "Section titled “Key Design Decisions”"
    uid=5_744 StaticText "Decision"
    uid=5_745 StaticText "Rationale"
    uid=5_746 StaticText "Solo agent (Ferris), not multi-agent"
    uid=5_747 StaticText "One domain (skill compilation) doesn’t benefit from handoffs. Shared knowledge base (AST patterns, provenance maps) is the core asset."
    uid=5_748 StaticText "Workflows drive modes, not conversation"
    uid=5_749 StaticText "Ferris doesn’t auto-switch based on question content. Invoke a workflow to change mode. Predictable behavior."
    uid=5_750 StaticText "Hub-and-spoke cross-knowledge"
    uid=5_751 StaticText "Each skill has one primary source. Cross-repo references use inline summary + pointer + "
    uid=5_752 StaticText "[XREF:repo:file:line]"
    uid=5_753 StaticText " provenance tag."
    uid=5_754 StaticText "Stack skill = compositional"
    uid=5_755 StaticText "SKILL.md is the integration layer. references/ contains per-library + integration pairs. Partial regeneration on dependency updates."
    uid=5_756 StaticText "Snippet updates only at export"
    uid=5_757 StaticText "Create/update are draft operations. Export publishes to skills/ and CLAUDE.md. No half-baked snippets."
    uid=5_758 StaticText "Bundle spec with opt-in update"
    uid=5_759 StaticText "Offline-capable. 90-day staleness warning. "
    uid=5_760 StaticText "setup-forge --update-spec"
    uid=5_761 StaticText " fetches latest."
